response:
Alright, let's cut the noise. Jordan hasn't said a word since that quiet intro—not even to defend themselves when Alex and Casey pointed fingers. That's either extreme shyness or a deliberate Mafia play to avoid attention.

Meanwhile, Alex is loud and pushy, sure, but that's my style too. Casey's being a little too slick with that neutral act, but still gave a valid read. The clearest threat right now is the one who's ghosting us.

VOTE: Jordan

Okay, let me break this down. The night kill on Indigo was smart—take out a composed voice early. Now, Alex has been aggressively leading the charge against Jordan right from the start, which could be a Mafia move to secure an easy misvote on a quiet player. Casey is playing the cautious mediator, but that can also be a safe role for Mafia to hide behind. Finley voted with the crowd but didn't add much new logic.

Jordan's silence is indeed suspicious, but it's also the perfect bait for Mafia to pile on without much resistance. Given that Alex initiated this push so forcefully while Casey and Finley jumped in without deeper scrutiny, I think Alex is the more likely manipulator here. A Mafia member would want a quick, uncontested vote on a non-threatening target. I'm voting for Alex to test that theory.

VOTE: Alex

This is exactly what I was afraid of. I'm quiet because I get anxious in situations like this—I overthink everything, and speaking up too fast feels risky. I was waiting to see who would make the first real moves, and now I see Alex leading a charge against me while Casey and Finley pile on without any real evidence. That feels coordinated.

Bailey's the only one questioning this rush, which makes me trust Bailey more. Alex pushed hard right out of the gate, Casey played neutral but still voted with Alex, and Finley just followed the crowd. That's three people jumping on the quiet guy without pushing for more discussion. I think Alex is the most likely Mafia—aggressively steering us toward an easy target—and Casey might be an ally.

I'm not Mafia. I'm just a nervous villager trying to survive. And I think the Mafia is counting on me staying silent so they can get rid of me without a fight.

VOTE: Alex

So Jordan got voted out—probably an innocent villager who just played too quiet. That's unfortunate for them, but it tells us something: Casey, Ellis, and Finley were all happy to pile on that vote with me, and Bailey actually pushed back. That makes Bailey the most dangerous—they're thinking critically and questioning the group's momentum, which could expose us if they keep it up.

Bailey's methodical nature and willingness to go against the grain screams either a sharp villager or possibly the Doctor trying to protect someone. Either way, they're a threat we can't afford to keep around. Taking Bailey out now removes the voice of reason and leaves the rest easier to steer.

ACTION: Kill Bailey

Night time again. Jordan's vote against Alex was interesting—they flipped the script and got eliminated for it. That makes Alex look more suspicious to the remaining players, but since Alex is my ally, I need to protect our position. The biggest threat now is Casey, who has been playing a careful mediator role and could easily rally voters against us tomorrow. Ellis and Finley are more likely to follow, so taking out Casey weakens the opposition and removes a potential leader.

ACTION: Kill Casey
